political.s: FEC: $25K PAC donations; 68% to Republican, 32% to Democratic committees.  [CA CalAccess + NY COELIG + TX Ethics adds state-level totals.]  [Stanford DIME (Bonica) corroborates.]

charity.s: $880M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.23% of revenue). JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #16 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. Drucker Institute Management Top 250 — ranked #2 (2024) on customer satisfaction, employee engagement, innovation, social responsibility, and financial strength. Fortune World's Most Admired Companies — ranked #1 (2024). Newsweek Most Trustworthy Companies in America — ranked #2 (2024).
charity.focus: Racial equity, education, housing, disaster relief, HIV/AIDS

environment.s: Science Based Targets initiative: approved. Emissions reduction targets independently validated. JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #16 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. CDP A-List (2024) — top tier for climate disclosure and emissions-reduction action. Climate-commitment coalition member: First Movers Coalition (since 2022) and RE100 (since 2016; target 2020). 100% renewable electricity globally — achieved 2020 (operations); 2030 target across full supply chain. Held by 7 ESG/SRI fund(s) (calvert, domini, ishares-esg, pax-world, tiaa-social-choice, trillium +1 more) — investor-screen positive signal.

labor.s: 31 federal penalties totaling $1.5B since 2000 (Violation Tracker). Supply-chain signals: KnowTheChain 47/100; CHRB 17/26; TVPRA exposure; UK MS statement; AU MS statement.

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. Disability:IN Equality Index score 80/100. JUST Capital JUST 100 — ranked #16 of Russell 1000 (2025) for treatment of workers, customers, communities, environment. Bloomberg Gender-Equality Index member (2024) — transparent on female leadership, equal pay, inclusive culture. As You Sow Gender Equality Funds — strong scores on pay equity + leadership. [as-you-sow-funds] HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

privacy.s: Common Sense Privacy: Pass rating on 1 product(s) (Apple TV+) — meets data-protection safeguards.

execPay.s: CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio 533:1 (median employee pay $512) — SEC proxy statement (DEF 14A, 2026).

wiki.parent: The Vanguard Group, Berkshire Hathaway, BlackRock, State Street Global Advisors
wiki.industry: software industry, consumer electronics industry, digital distribution, electronics industry, information technology industry, mobile phone industry, software development
wiki.keyPeople: Steve Wozniak, Ronald Wayne, Steve Jobs
wiki.controversies:
=== Criticism and controversies ===

Apple has faced criticism regarding its market practices, labor conditions, and privacy policies. The company has been criticized for alleged anti-competitive behavior
 and its alleged participation in the PRISM surveillance program. Significant concerns have been raised about labor issues in its supply chain, restrictions on the right to repair, and the company's tax strategies and environmental impact. Furthermore, Apple has faced scrutiny for its business operations in nations with restrictive human rights records, such as China.

On May 5, 2026, Apple settled for $250 million a shareholder lawsuit after the lawsuit was filed by Peter Landsheft in US federal court in California in 2024 over delays in AI upgrades to Siri.
wiki.extract: Apple Inc. is an American multinational technology company headquartered in Cupertino, California, in Silicon Valley, and known for consumer electronics, software and online services. Founded in 1976 as Apple Computer Company by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne, the company was incorporated by Jobs and Wozniak as Apple Computer, Inc. the following year. Its name was changed to its current one in 2007 as the company expanded its focus from computers to consumer electronics. Apple is one of the Big Tech companies.

health.s: 16 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: an internal short can cause the battery cells to overheat.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Improper Locking Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Apple watchOS, iOS, iPadOS, macOS, visionOS, and tvOS contain an improper locking vulnerability that could allow a malicious application to cause unexpected changes in memory shared between processes.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Classic Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Apple watchOS, iOS, iPadOS, macOS, visionOS, and tvOS contain a classic buffer overflow vulnerability which could allow a malicious application to cause unexpected system termination or write kernel memory.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Apple Safari, iOS, watchOS, visionOS, iPadOS, macOS, and tvOS contain a buffer overflow vulnerability that could allow the processing of maliciously crafted web content which may lead to memory corruption.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

topCves.shortDescription: Apple macOS, iOS, iPadOS, and Safari 16.6 contain a use-after-free vulnerability due to the processing of maliciously crafted web content that may lead to memory corruption.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

topCves.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Integer Overflow or Wraparound Vulnerability
topCves.shortDescription: Apple tvOS, macOS, Safari, iPadOS and watchOS contain an integer overflow or wraparound vulnerability due to the processing of maliciously crafted web content that may lead to arbitrary code execution.
topCves.requiredAction: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

highestSeverity.vulnerabilityName: Apple Multiple Products Improper Locking Vulnerability
Apple Multiple Products Classic Buffer Overflow Vulnerability

sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee had been sitting in a truck that was not on and had no air conditioning. The employee felt ill and was hospitalized for heat illness.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was using a hex key to perform equipment changes on a core shooter machine when the machine cycled and crushed their leg, resulting in a broken leg.

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by object held or wielded by person
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cooking orders on the cook's line and preparing bacon for an order. The employee was chopping bacon with a chef's knife when their right middle finger was partially amputated by the knife.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking down the hallway when her foot hit a sticky spot or got caught on the ground and she fell onto her left side. She was hospitalized for a fractured left femur.

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was using a plastic pipe to clear a jam in the shredding equipment. The pipe got stuck in the shredder and went into a vertical position, crushing the employee's left ring finger between the pipe and the top of the shredder shroud. The employee sustained a fingertip amputation.

topHazards.label: An internal short can cause the battery cells to overheat, posing a fire hazard to consumers.
The recalled shell fireworks can explode prematurely, posing explosion and burn hazards to consumers and bystanders.
The batteries in the recalled laptop computers can overheat, posing a fire hazard.
The three-prong AC wall plug adapters can break and expose the metal portion of the adapter, posing an electric shock risk.
The metal blade of the slicer can detach, posing a laceration hazard to the consumer.

sampleRecalls.title: 13131 Imports Recalls Red Apple Fireworks Brand "American Glory" and "Merica AF" Fireworks Due to Explosion and Burn Hazards; Violation of Federal Firework Safety Standards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves two different Red Apple Fireworks reloadable tube artillery shell fireworks. The American Glory firework contains 24 shells and was sold in red, white and blue packaging featuring a cartoon depiction of an eagle. The 'Merica AF firework contains six shells and was sold in red, white and blue packa…
hazards: The recalled shell fireworks can explode prematurely, posing explosion and burn hazards to consumers and bystanders.
injuries: The firm has received 72 reports of fireworks prematurely exploding. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Apple Recalls 15-Inch MacBook Pro Laptop Computers Due to Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves certain Apple 15-inch MacBook Pro laptop computers. The recalled laptop computers have a 15.4-inch (diagonal) display, 2.2-2.5 GHz processors, 256GB-1TB solid-state storage, two Thunderbolt 2 ports, two USB 3 ports, and one HDMI port. Consumers can determine if their laptop computer is included in…
hazards: The batteries in the recalled laptop computers can overheat, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: Apple has received 26 reports of the laptop's battery overheating, including five reports of minor burns and one report of smoke inhalation, as well as 17 reports of minor damage to nearby personal p…
units: About 432,000 (in addition, about 26,000 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Apple Recalls Three-Prong Wall Plug Adapters Included in World Travel Adapter Kit Due to Risk of Electric Shock
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves three-prong AC wall plug adapters included in the Apple World Travel Adapter Kit sold before February 2015. The kits contain three-prong and two-prong AC wall plug adapters that fit different electrical outlets worldwide. The recalled adapters were made for use primarily in the United Kingdom, Sin…
hazards: The three-prong AC wall plug adapters can break and expose the metal portion of the adapter, posing an electric shock risk.
injuries: Apple has received six reports from other countries of wall plug adapters breaking and consumers receiving shocks, including two reports of consumers who were medically evaluated and released without…
products: Apple AC World Travel Adapter Kits
units: About 814,000 (in addition, about 81,000 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Far East Brokers Recalls Pineapple Corer & Slicers Due to Laceration Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Pineapple Corer & Slicers sold under Fabulous Home brand. The corer & slicer is white with a black top and made mostly from plastic with a metal blade. It works much like a cork screw to core and slice pineapples.
hazards: The metal blade of the slicer can detach, posing a laceration hazard to the consumer.
injuries: Far East has received one report of the metal blade becoming detached. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Waupaca Elevator Recalls to Inspect Elevators Due to Injury Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves residential elevator models Custom Lift 450# and Custom Lift 500#, shipped and installed between 1979 and 2008. The recalled elevators are used in consumers' homes. The Custom Lift 450# elevators have serial numbers 10-1001 through 10-3131. The Custom Lift 500# elevators have serial numbers 10-313…
hazards: The elevator cab can fall unexpectedly to the bottom of the elevator shaft and abruptly stop, posing an injury hazard to consumers in the elevator cab.
injuries: The firm has received 12 reports of the elevator cab falling unexpectedly, including 10 reported injuries that resulted in strained or sprained knees and ankles, and broken bones.
